commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The current weather condition icon/emoji is missing from the main header, despite being a core requirement of the prompt.
issues.fix: Add a prominent icon placeholder or map the 'condition' string to an emoji/icon alongside the temperature display.

issues.description: The component lacks any hover or active states for the forecast items, which typically act as interactive elements in weather widgets.
issues.fix: Add subtle hover states to forecast items to indicate potential clickability if the user intends to drill down into daily details.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and makes good use of the design system primitives. It misses a key visual requirement—the main weather icon—but otherwise provides a solid, accessible implementation of a weather widget.

evaluation.critique: The component has good data handling and hierarchy but inconsistently applies design tokens—it mixes CSS variables with hardcoded fallback hex colors and lacks spacing token usage throughout. Polish is lacking with no hover/focus states, transitions, or loading indicators for the forecast section.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.fix: Add a legend section below the search bar mapping categories to their colors.

issues.description: Missing hover states on grid cells.
issues.fix: Add a hover effect (e.g., opacity change or border highlight) to the grid cells to indicate interactivity.
Use a button element or add tabIndex={0} and keydown handlers to the grid cells to make them accessible via keyboard.

issues.description: Grid cells feel flat without active state styling.
issues.fix: Apply a distinct border or shadow styling to the currently selected element to provide visual feedback.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and uses CSS Grid effectively for the periodic table layout. It needs improved accessibility for keyboard users and the inclusion of the requested legend to meet all requirements.

evaluation.critique: The component properly uses CSS Grid for the 18-column layout and design tokens for most spacing/colors, but has critical issues: filtered elements break grid positioning (gaps appear when elements are hidden), no legend is rendered despite being promised, and missing interactive polish like hover states, transitions, and focus indicators on clickable cells.

commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

issues.description: The main weather condition display is missing the requested icon/emoji.
issues.fix: Add a prominent emoji or icon next to the current condition text or temperature.

issues.description: The card has a hover interaction (scale) but no equivalent focus state or keyboard accessibility.
issues.fix: Add tabIndex={0} to the wrapper and apply the scale effect on :focus-within or :focus-visible using CSS.

issues.description: Using React state (isHovered) for a simple hover transform causes unnecessary re-renders.
issues.fix: Replace the onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave logic with a CSS :hover selector on the wrapper div.

evaluations.critique: The component is visually polished and uses the design system primitives effectively to create a clear hierarchy. It handles prop defaults and null safety well, but it missed the specific requirement to include an icon for the main weather condition and lacks keyboard accessibility for its hover effect.

evaluation.critique: The component has good hierarchy and data handling, but uses hardcoded default forecast data in props (violating the requirement), mixes design token references with fallback hex colors inconsistently, and includes inline pixel values (24, 20, 16, 12, 8px) instead of using spacing tokens throughout.

issues.description: The component code is truncated and incomplete, ending abruptly in the Detail Panel section.
issues.fix: Complete the Detail Panel implementation and close all open JSX tags and the component function.

issues.description: The 'atomicWeight' field requested in the prompt is missing from the detail panel (likely due to the truncation).
issues.fix: Add a text display for atomicWeight in the detail panel stack.

issues.description: The grid uses a hardcoded 900px min-width which might be too wide for some container contexts despite the overflow wrapper.
issues.fix: Consider using a more fluid min-width or container-relative sizing for the grid cells.

evaluations.critique: The component demonstrates excellent UX logic by using opacity and pointer-events to filter elements while maintaining the table's structural integrity. Accessibility is handled very well with grid roles and keyboard listeners; however, the provided source code is incomplete and fails to implement the atomicWeight requirement due to being cut off.

evaluation.critique: The component has good responsive structure with CSS Grid and proper element positioning, but uses some hardcoded pixel values (900px minWidth) instead of spacing tokens. While it correctly applies design tokens for colors and spacing, the categoryColors object contains fallback hex values and rgba patterns that shouldn't be there—all colors should be pure CSS variables. The search/filter functionality and detail panel work well with proper data handling, though the legend display is cut off in the provided code.
